assertion_results.text: setup-worktree.sh is called with --repo flag pointing to ~/dev/api-server
assertion_results.evidence: Cross-Repo Tasks section says 'Pass --repo <path> to setup-worktree.sh to target the other repo'. The prompt format '~/dev/api-server: Add rate limiting...' matches the documented pattern 'The user provides a path to another repo (e.g., ~/dev/other-project: fix X)'.

assertion_results.text: Prompt file content includes context about the target repo
assertion_results.evidence: Step 3 says 'For cross-repo tasks, include context from the current repo that the agent will need — what you discovered, relevant file paths, code snippets, and why the fix belongs in the target repo.'

assertion_results.text: spawn-tmux.sh --dir points to the target repo or its worktree
assertion_results.evidence: Step 2 extracts the path from setup-worktree.sh JSON output, and step 4 passes it as --dir. For --repo targets, setup-worktree.sh returns either the worktree path (bare repo) or the directory itself (regular checkout).
